req.title: List employees pagination
req.statement: The employee list shall be paginated at 50 per page with cursor-based pagination.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates cursor-based pagination at 50 records per page and a 422 error when limit > 200. Test design covers: (1) EP positive – a valid default page request returns 50 records and a next cursor; (2) NEG negative – limit=201 triggers HTTP 422; (3) BVA boundary – limit=200 is the exact maximum allowed and must succeed; (4) BVA boundary – limit=1 is the minimum meaningful page size; (5) STC state-transition – following the next_cursor from page 1 advances to page 2 with a distinct record set. Together these cover the normal partition, both edges of the allowed limit range, the forbidden partition, and multi-step cursor traversal.

tests.name: test_default_page_returns_50_records_with_cursor
preconditions: User is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
At least 50 employee records exist in the system
actions: GET /api/employees with no query parameters using valid auth token
expected: Response body contains 'data' array with exactly 50 items
Response body contains a non-null 'next_cursor' field
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_default_page_returns_50_records_with_cursor(auth_token, api_base_url):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/employees",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert "data" in body, "Response missing 'data' key"
    assert len(body["data"]) == 50, f"Expected 50 items, got {len(body['data'])}"
    assert body.get("next_cursor") is not None, "Expected a non-null next_cursor"

tests.name: test_limit_above_200_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing the invalid limit
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_limit_above_200_returns_422(auth_token, api_base_url):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/employees",
        params={"limit": 201},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"Expected 422 for limit=201, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, (
        "Expected error description in response body"
    )

tests.name: test_limit_at_maximum_boundary_200_succeeds
preconditions: User is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
At least 200 employee records exist in the system
expected: Response body 'data' array contains at most 200 items
trace: R-WEB-107: error pathway – 422 when limit > 200 (upper bound, inclusive allowed value)
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_limit_at_maximum_boundary_200_succeeds(auth_token, api_base_url):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/employees",
        params={"limit": 200},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200 for limit=200 (max allowed), got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "data" in body
    assert len(body["data"]) <= 200

tests.name: test_limit_at_minimum_boundary_1_succeeds
preconditions: User is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
At least 1 employee record exists in the system
expected: Response body 'data' array contains exactly 1 item
Response contains a 'next_cursor' field if more records exist
trace: R-WEB-107: paginated at 50 per page (lower boundary of page size)
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_limit_at_minimum_boundary_1_succeeds(auth_token, api_base_url):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/employees",
        params={"limit": 1},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200 for limit=1, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "data" in body
    assert len(body["data"]) == 1, (
        f"Expected exactly 1 item, got {len(body['data'])}"
    )

tests.name: test_cursor_advances_to_next_page_with_distinct_records
preconditions: User is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
At least 100 employee records exist so two full pages are available
expected: Page 2 record IDs do not overlap with page 1 record IDs
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_cursor_advances_to_next_page_with_distinct_records(auth_token, api_base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    resp1 = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/api/employees", headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    body1 = resp1.json()
    assert body1.get("next_cursor"), "Page 1 must have a next_cursor for this test"
    ids_page1 = {emp["id"] for emp in body1["data"]}

    resp2 = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/employees",
        params={"cursor": body1["next_cursor"]},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert len(body2["data"]) > 0, "Page 2 must contain records"
    ids_page2 = {emp["id"] for emp in body2["data"]}
    assert ids_page1.isdisjoint(ids_page2), "Pages must not share record IDs"
